rows.description: The usual European Union plates with PL written on them:.
White centreline with white or no sideline:. Double centreline appears only near junctions and with different overtaking rules for the lanes:.
Only one bollard type, the white bollard with a red stripe going all around:.
Northern Poland has this specific architectural style – tall slim houses with steep roofs (with or without ornamentation):.

rows.indicator: speed limit signs have a characteristic font to them
rows.description: The speed limit signs have a characteristic font to them:. Large crosses like this by the side of the road can be found in Poland, Czechia, Slovakia and Romania and serve as a memorial to a person who lost their life there:. Similar memorials can be found in other countries around too, but usually not with crosses this large.

rows.indicator: Warning signs in Poland have a yellow background
rows.description: Warning signs in Poland have a yellow background, which is a feature of only a few countries in Europe, and a thin frame line:. Poland has these distinctive settlement entrance and exit signs with a town outline depicted on them:. The turn arrows are red with a white background:. These signs are quite common on Polish roads:.
The European Larch grows around Poland too, in addition to the Alps and Carpathian mountain ranges:.
Poland has these characteristic slim holed poles:.
Green signs with white text, except on highways:. Highway signs are blue with white text, except when pointing towards non-highway roads:.

rows.indicator: Rural roadside conventions — Road numbering system
rows.description: 3-digit national roads are grouped together and their first digit zones range from 1 in the northwest to 9 in the southeast. More info on the Polish road numbering system:. Poland Road Numbering System.

warnings: Local deterministic extraction used GeoTips text only; associated images were not interpreted, so descriptions contain no image-derived details.
